campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's money management for July 2025, including how investments performed and overall budget status. They also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to make it easier for residents to get around using different methods beyond just cars.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and city finances
Monthly budget and treasurer's report review
New transportation plan focusing on multiple ways to travel around Campbell

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new local law) and skipped the normal first reading requirement to speed up the process. The ordinance is now scheduled to be adopted at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing conducted to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for approval
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular meeting
First reading requirement waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda focused on administrative housekeeping and moving forward with a construction project, though the specific details of the construction work aren't fully outlined in the available information.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute contracts
Review of closed session meetings from March

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 8, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming council sessions. They reviewed and finalized the agenda for April 14, 2026, and looked ahead to the April 21, 2026 meeting agenda, deciding which items to add and whether any topics needed special time slots. The council also reviewed public records from late March through early April.
keyTopics: Finalized April 14, 2026 council meeting agenda
Reviewed draft agenda for April 21, 2026 meeting
Scheduled specific time slots for agenda items as needed
Reviewed public records from March 26 - April 2, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate either in person or online via Zoom. Residents who wanted to speak during the meeting could join the virtual meeting and use the 'raise hand' feature to request their turn to comment.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access available via Zoom
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Phone dial-in option for those without internet

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their April 7, 2026 meeting where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and recognized two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The council used this meeting to highlight and celebrate these community observances.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
City Council special order of business

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives including a food and budget program presentation, hear from a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and learn about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved minutes from their previous March meeting and opened the floor for residents to speak about any local issues for three minutes each.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth organization update
Summer job opportunities for Cupertino residents
Public comment period on local issues

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person and remote options so residents could participate however works best for them. The city is making it easy for people to attend and share their views either by showing up in person, joining online via Zoom, or submitting written comments before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation options for residents
Written public comment submissions accepted before meetings
Multiple ways to join: Zoom link, phone dial-in, or in-person attendance

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review several administrative and planning matters. Key items included updates to the city's long-term housing and community development plan, new project requests from individual council members, and a proposed change to how the city fills council vacancies.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New project requests submitted by council members
Changes to the council vacancy appointment process
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling updates

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's gas utility budget and rates for the next fiscal year. The main decision involved reviewing financial forecasts, moving money between accounts, and adjusting how much residents and businesses will pay for natural gas service.
keyTopics: Gas utility rates increasing for residential and commercial customers
Financial forecast and budget planning for FY 2027
Reserve fund transfers to support utility operations
Three different rate schedules being adjusted (residential, master-metered, and large commercial)
